ooc_tweet.text: After criticism, the National Park Service has restored an old Underground Railroad webpage that prominently features Harriet Tubman

The deadline for using a REAL ID for travel is less than a month away.

If you haven't gotten your REAL ID license yet, here's what to know as the deadline approaches:
A 19-year-old Asian elephant at the Zurich Zoo gave birth this weekend to a male calf whose name, starting with the letter Z, will be announced this week.
A Michigan bookstore owner is speaking out after hundreds of community members helped move books by hand to a new storefront.
Parents of children with autism are speaking out against recent comments made by HHS Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr., saying his remarks about autism and vaccines reinforce harmful stereotypes.
New research released shows a new, oral GLP-1 pill may be just as beneficial to those with Type 2 diabetes as injectable GLP-1 drugs.

Latest round of hostage releases begins as Israeli soldier Agam Berger, 20, is handed to Red Cross in Gaza. Follow live updates.
Eight hostages due to be freed Thursday, including 3 Israelis and 5 Thais, have been released. Israel condemned the chaotic handover scenes.

Hamas has handed over three hostages, including an Israeli-American hostage and two other hostages earlier, in the latest round of releases under the ceasefire deal. Israel is expected to release nearly 200 Palestinian prisoners. Follow live updates.
Hamas fires its first missiles at Israel since a ceasefire collapsed earlier this week as new Israeli strikes overnight killed dozens of people
President Trump says the US "will take over" the Gaza Strip, after earlier saying he doesn't think there is a permanent future for Palestinians in Gaza
Classes are set to resume on Monday at Florida State University after Thursday's deadly mass shooting. FSU says it is waiving all mandatory attendance policies that could affect any students' grades.

Sen. Chris Van Hollen slammed El Salvador President Nayib Bukele and President Donald Trump for what he said was a setup to defame him and Kilmar Abrego Garcia, the man at the center of an erroneous deportation, during their meeting in El Salvador.

Current and former Seattle police officers who attended President Trump's "Stop the Steal" political rally on Jan. 6, 2021, at the U.S. Capitol are asking the nation's highest court to keep their identities anonymous in public court records.
